comments.body:
Hello Shawn,

FedEx One Rate is a flat-rate shipping option, which means the shipping cost remains the same regardless of the destination, as long as the shipment meets the required packaging and size criteria.

For example, if a package has a cubic size of 120 cubic inches, it may qualify for a FedEx Envelope with a fixed price of $30.67.
With FedEx One Rate:

- Shipping within North Carolina will cost $30.67
- Shipping from North Carolina to Alaska will also cost $30.67
This is why it is called a flat rate — the price does not change based on distance.

Without FedEx One Rate:

- Shipping costs are calculated based on distance, weight, and dimensions
- So, shipping from North Carolina to Alaska would typically be more expensive than shipping within the same state

If you have any further questions, we recommend checking with your FedEx account representative for more details.

Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello,

Following guidance from BigCommerce, we are considering setting each product’s dimensions to match the actual shipping box used in fulfillment (rather than the product’s physical size).

Is this the recommended approach when using PluginHive, and is this a common approach PluginHive users typically follow for accurate real-time shipping rates?

Specifically, our plan would be to:

-

Set each product’s dimensions to match the shipping box

-

Include total shipment weight (product + packaging)

-

Avoid any system-generated packing or stacking logic

In addition, we have some smaller items (such as bracelets) that typically ship in padded envelopes. If a customer orders multiple bracelets, we would not want to charge shipping as if each item requires its own envelope.

Can rules be configured so that:

-

Smaller items can be combined into a single package/envelope when appropriate

-

Shipping dimensions reflect the actual packaging used (rather than multiplying envelopes per item)

Before we move forward, can you please confirm:

1.

Will this approach prevent the system from over-calculating package dimensions when multiple quantities are ordered?

2.

How are multiple quantities of the same product handled — as separate packages or combined?

3.

Are there rules or settings available to control when items are combined vs. shipped individually?

4.

Are there any additional settings within PluginHive we should enable or disable to support this setup?

5.

Lastly, how do we ensure only our selected shipping service(s) appear at checkout?

Our goal is to align the system with how our products are actually packed and shipped in real-world fulfillment.

comments.html_body:
Hello,

Following guidance from BigCommerce, we are considering setting each product’s dimensions to match the actual shipping box used in fulfillment (rather than the product’s physical size).

Is this the recommended approach when using PluginHive, and is this a common approach PluginHive users typically follow for accurate real-time shipping rates?

Specifically, our plan would be to:

  • Set each product’s dimensions to match the shipping box

  • Include total shipment weight (product + packaging)

  • Avoid any system-generated packing or stacking logic

In addition, we have some smaller items (such as bracelets) that typically ship in padded envelopes. If a customer orders multiple bracelets, we would not want to charge shipping as if each item requires its own envelope.

Can rules be configured so that:

  • Smaller items can be combined into a single package/envelope when appropriate

  • Shipping dimensions reflect the actual packaging used (rather than multiplying envelopes per item)

Before we move forward, can you please confirm:

  1. Will this approach prevent the system from over-calculating package dimensions when multiple quantities are ordered?

  2. How are multiple quantities of the same product handled — as separate packages or combined?

  3. Are there rules or settings available to control when items are combined vs. shipped individually?

  4. Are there any additional settings within PluginHive we should enable or disable to support this setup?

  5. Lastly, how do we ensure only our selected shipping service(s) appear at checkout?

Our goal is to align the system with how our products are actually packed and shipped in real-world fulfillment.

Thank you for your guidance.

Best regards,
Shawn

events.body:
Hello Shawn,

FedEx One Rate is a flat-rate shipping option, which means the shipping cost remains the same regardless of the destination, as long as the shipment meets the required packaging and size criteria.

For example, if a package has a cubic size of 120 cubic inches, it may qualify for a FedEx Envelope with a fixed price of $30.67.
With FedEx One Rate:

- Shipping within North Carolina will cost $30.67
- Shipping from North Carolina to Alaska will also cost $30.67
This is why it is called a flat rate — the price does not change based on distance.

Without FedEx One Rate:

- Shipping costs are calculated based on distance, weight, and dimensions
- So, shipping from North Carolina to Alaska would typically be more expensive than shipping within the same state

If you have any further questions, we recommend checking with your FedEx account representative for more details.

Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

Your request ({{ticket.id}}) has been updated. To add additional comments, reply to this email.
{{ticket.comments_formatted}}

Hello,

Following guidance from BigCommerce, we are considering setting each product’s dimensions to match the actual shipping box used in fulfillment (rather than the product’s physical size).

Is this the recommended approach when using PluginHive, and is this a common approach PluginHive users typically follow for accurate real-time shipping rates?

Specifically, our plan would be to:

-

Set each product’s dimensions to match the shipping box

-

Include total shipment weight (product + packaging)

-

Avoid any system-generated packing or stacking logic

In addition, we have some smaller items (such as bracelets) that typically ship in padded envelopes. If a customer orders multiple bracelets, we would not want to charge shipping as if each item requires its own envelope.

Can rules be configured so that:

-

Smaller items can be combined into a single package/envelope when appropriate

-

Shipping dimensions reflect the actual packaging used (rather than multiplying envelopes per item)

Before we move forward, can you please confirm:

1.

Will this approach prevent the system from over-calculating package dimensions when multiple quantities are ordered?

2.

How are multiple quantities of the same product handled — as separate packages or combined?

3.

Are there rules or settings available to control when items are combined vs. shipped individually?

4.

Are there any additional settings within PluginHive we should enable or disable to support this setup?

5.

Lastly, how do we ensure only our selected shipping service(s) appear at checkout?

Our goal is to align the system with how our products are actually packed and shipped in real-world fulfillment.

Thank you for your guidance.

Best regards,
Shawn

I see it in the blog - https://share.zight.com/kpu2Wook
-
Is it common for users (Big Commerce/ PluginHive to set products the same size as a box to limit shipping issues?

To confirm our direction, we are considering moving forward by setting each product’s dimensions to match the actual shipping box used in fulfillment, along with the total weight (including packaging).

Additionally, we are using One Rate shipping, so as long as the shipment remains within the required box size and under 50 lbs, the price remains consistent. This should help simplify the setup on our end.

One follow-up question:

For smaller items (such as bracelets), where multiple units typically ship together in a single padded envelope, is there any recommended approach to help prevent the system from overestimating packaging when multiple quantities are added? For example, if items fit within a single envelope, can this be handled more by total weight and size rather than treating each item as a separate package?

Our goal is to keep shipping rates as close as possible to real-world fulfillment while maintaining a simple and consistent setup.

Thanks again for your help, and we’ll begin testing on our end as well.

Best regards,
Shawn
